comma.description: Short pause. Like a quick breath.
comma.example: She smiled, but her eyes didn’t.

em_dash.description: Medium pause. Used for emphasis or shift in tone.

ellipsis.description: Long, soft pause. Indicates hesitation or trailing off.
ellipsis.example: I thought I was ready… but maybe not.
ellipsis.how_to_read: Pause gently after 'ready', sound unsure or distant.

period.description: Full stop. End of a sentence.
period.how_to_read: Pause fully between sentences. Reset tone.

line_break.description: Longest pause. Major beat or dramatic moment.
line_break.example:
He opened the door.

She was gone.
line_break.how_to_read: Pause long before second line. Read it slowly and dramatically.

exclamation.description: Strong or urgent tone. Emotional or loud.
exclamation.how_to_read: Shout or project voice. Pause afterward.

question.description: Rising tone. Sounds like you're asking.
question.how_to_read: Raise tone at end of sentence, then pause.

italics.description: Soft emphasis. Internal thoughts, sarcasm, or contrast.
italics.how_to_read: Use a more emotional, quiet, or sarcastic tone.

bold.how_to_read: Say with more force or conviction than normal tone.

bold_italics_caps.description: Yelling, intense emotion, or dramatic breakdown.
bold_italics_caps.how_to_read: Shout or deliver with extreme urgency. Pause afterward.

stretched_words.description: Repeated letters mean the word is stretched for emotion.
stretched_words.how_to_read: Recognize the normal word, then stretch it slightly. Don't spell out each letter.

reading_instructions.final_tip: Read like a human. Pause naturally. Let emotional beats land with silence or slower pacing.
